included: ylong_http_client async HTTP/1 response-head read loop
MixStream and ProxyStream nested TLS layering
AsyncSslStream and OpenSSL wrapper read path
HTTP/1 pool checkout and dispatcher occupancy
request build/format and response body drain path
excluded: benchmark-only runtime tuning as SOTA evidence
modifying ylong_runtime dependency internals outside the allowed ylong_http_client source scope
changing HTTP proxy or TLS protocol semantics

verdict.summary: No production-grade change inside ylong_http_client was found that plausibly reduces the failing nested HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy response_read latency by >=30% without changing protocol semantics. The remaining high-leverage causal pointer is ylong_runtime readiness/parking behavior around nested TLS I/O, not request formatting, pool dispatch, response-head parsing, stream enum layering, or OpenSSL wrapper call count.

supporting_evidence.claim: HTTP/1 response-head loop is already one poll_fn around one raw poll_read attempt per poll; measured response-read churn is not high.
supporting_evidence.observed: Nested HTTPS-origin proxy cells show about 2 response_read polls and 1 pending per request while response_read remains about 1.0-1.3ms/request.

supporting_evidence.claim: Nested stream layering is not an untested high-leverage candidate.
code_refs: ylong_http_client/src/async_impl/mix.rs: ProxyStream and MixStream read/write delegation
supporting_evidence.observed: The dedicated nested TLS stream variant was already tested and rejected.

supporting_evidence.claim: TLS wrapper call amplification is not the measured root cause.
supporting_evidence.observed: Corrected diagnostics show nested HTTPS-origin cells around 5 SSL_read calls and 2 SSL-read pendings per request; the progress-aware retry candidate saw zero pending-after-progress events and was rejected.

supporting_evidence.claim: Pool, request build/format, and body drain are too small to explain a SOTA-scale change.
supporting_evidence.observed: H1 pool checkout is about 3us/request, request build/format are microsecond scale, and body_drain is about 1.5us/request in the failing concurrency diagnostic, versus response_read about 1.0-1.3ms/request.

supporting_evidence.claim: Server/proxy send and relay timings are insufficient root cause.
supporting_evidence.observed: Extra server-side send/relay work is tens to low hundreds of microseconds per request, while ylong response_read is about 1.26-1.31ms/request in failing nested cells.

supporting_evidence.claim: The strongest remaining causal pointer is runtime readiness/parking, outside ylong_http_client internals.
supporting_evidence.observed: strace diagnostic shows comparable recvfrom/sendto counts versus libcurl but much higher futex, clock_nanosleep, epoll_wait, and sched_yield activity for ylong.

decision.reason: A new ylong_http_client production patch would not be evidence-led under the fixed SOTA target. Remaining investigation must either produce reviewed permission to work below ylong_http_client in runtime/readiness behavior, or produce a new predeclared S9 hypothesis with evidence that contradicts this hard-negative review.
decision.next_action: Record this as S9 causal narrowing evidence, keep S9 active, and do not run S10 or final goal_achieved gate.
